ops.title: TERMINAL MAÎTRE · StadiaChat

ops.assignVolunteers: Assigner des bénévoles (facultatif — envoie des cartes tâche)

ops.noCritical: Aucun message Catégorie D pour le moment.

fan.title: Assistance vocale fan
fan.subtitle: Dirigez le micro vers le fan. L'IA détecte la langue, répond à partir des protocoles du stade et lit la réponse à voix haute.
fan.autoSpeak: Lire la réponse à voix haute
fan.coachingTitle: Assistance bénévole requise — vos actions (non lues)
fan.typeFallback: Ou tapez la question du fan si le micro est indisponible…
fan.unsupported: Ce navigateur ne prend pas en charge la reconnaissance vocale. Utilisez Chrome/Edge en HTTPS ou localhost, ou tapez la question du fan ci-dessous.
fan.noSpeech: Aucune parole capturée. Maintenez le micro et réessayez.
fan.micDenied: Permission micro refusée. Autorisez l'accès au micro dans le navigateur.
fan.notSecureTitle: Micro bloqué sur cette URL (Non sécurisé)
fan.notSecure: Les navigateurs n'autorisent le micro que sur https:// ou http://localhost — pas les IP LAN comme 192.168.x.x. Ouvrez http://localhost:3000 pour le micro, ou tapez la question du fan ci-dessous (le texte fonctionne toujours).
home.tagline: Coupe du Monde FIFA 2026 · IA ops stade
home.badge: Canal opérationnel sécurisé · Pas un chatbot public pour fans
home.hero1: IA pour le personnel du stade qui
home.hero2: les opérations du jour de match
home.heroBody: StadiaChat est l'interface ops AI Core pour les bénévoles et Operations Leads de site de la Coupe du Monde FIFA 2026. Elle répond instantanément aux FAQ de protocole, escalade les incidents au bon Lead, déploie les SOP d'urgence sans latence et aide les bénévoles à assister des fans multilingues via micro et haut-parleur — le tout isolé par stade.
home.sessionBound: Session liée à stadium_id · PIN · rôle
home.regNoPin: Pas de PIN du stade nécessaire pour s'inscrire. Après l'approbation de votre Ops Lead, connectez-vous avec le PIN du stade.
home.demoRegHint: En attente jusqu'à l'approbation de l'Ops Lead — puis connexion avec PIN.
home.demoOpsHint: metlife_2026 · ops_metlife_2026 · langue enregistrée sur le Lead
home.aboutTitle: Conçu pour le chaos du jour de match — pas pour des démos de chatbot génériques
home.purposeBody: Donner à chaque bénévole du stade une interface opérationnelle rapide et contextuelle : protocoles officiels dans leur langue, escalade claire vers l'Operations Lead, et chemins de sécurité critiques qui n'attendent jamais un canal radio saturé. Un Lead par stade gère un terminal maître pour les approbations, tâches et contrôle des incidents.
home.not1: Pas un chatbot public de tourisme ou de guide de la ville
home.not2: Pas un remplacement du commandement médical/sécurité d'urgence
home.not3: Pas un chat social libre entre fans
home.not4: Pas autorisé à utiliser cartes ou listes d'un autre stadium_id
home.catADesc: Réponse officielle de la bibliothèque locale du stade, localisée.
home.catBDesc: Rejet ferme → app Fan Guide. Pas de conseils libres sur la ville.
home.catCDesc: Carte d'alerte à l'Ops Lead + 3 cases de remédiation.
home.catDDesc: SOP d'urgence ou alerte Lead + contournement de sécurité 300s.
home.featuresTitle: Flux du jour de match de bout en bout
home.feat1Body: Every session is locked to a stadium_id, PIN, and role. No cross-venue data bleed. Six WC2026 venues share one codebase with venue-specific landmarks.
home.feat2Body: Protocol FAQs (A), city out-of-scope reject (B), minor floor issues to Ops Lead (C), or critical emergency SOPs + 300s safety override (D). Protocol match first; GenAI when needed.
home.feat3Body: Approve volunteers, planning board (gates/sections), task cards, 3-option remediations, and serious alerts with a 300s safety override if the Lead does not act.
home.feat4Body: Mic + speaker for multilingual fans. Spoken protocol answers; coaches the volunteer in chat when escort or hands-on help is required. Fans never log in.
home.faqIntro: Product answers for judges, construction & deploy answers for engineers. Full written guide: docs/FAQ.md.
home.ja1: World Cup venues run thousands of multilingual volunteers. Radio and ad-hoc chat are noisy, unsafe for emergencies, and can leak data across sites. StadiaChat is a tenancy-safe AI ops channel: official SOP answers instantly, escalates only what needs a human Lead, deploys emergency protocols under pressure, and helps volunteers assist fans in their language via Fan Voice.
home.ja2: FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium volunteers and one Operations Lead per stadium — not a public fan chatbot. Fans are helped through the volunteer’s device (mic + speaker). Six demo venues are seeded (MetLife, SoFi, AT&T, BC Place, Azteca, Hard Rock).
home.jq3: What should I demo in 2 minutes?
home.ja3: 1) Volunteer login: Alex Rivera · metlife_2026 · PIN WC26-MET. 2) Ask: “Where is the ADA elevator to Gate 3?” (Cat A protocol FAQ). 3) Report: “Low brochure inventory at Gate 1” (Cat C → Ops Lead). 4) Ops Lead: metlife_2026 · ops_metlife_2026 — approve remediations. 5) Fan Voice: Spanish facility question → spoken answer + volunteer coaching when needed.
home.jq4: How does safety work for critical incidents?
home.ja4: Category D: if a matching emergency protocol exists (medical, missing child, fire, unattended bag, etc.), it deploys to the volunteer immediately and bypasses the Lead. If not, the Lead gets a critical alert + 3 options and a 300-second timer. No Lead action → automatic safety directive to the volunteer.
home.jq5: What stadium protocol FAQs can volunteers ask?
home.ja5: Sixteen Cat A topics per stadium (same topics, venue landmarks differ): ADA/elevators, restrooms, tickets, food/concessions, merch, guest services, water/ATM/Wi‑Fi/prayer, medical posts, prohibited items, re-entry, smoking, queues, accessibility+, radio channels, shift/credential, zone access. Eleven Cat D emergencies (medical, missing child, crush, water hazard, fire, bag, fight, pitch invasion, active threat, weather, structural). Full list: docs/PROTOCOL_PACK.md.
home.dq1: How do I run it locally (construction)?
home.da1: See docs/SETUP.md. Node 20+: git clone → npm install → copy .env.example to .env.local → set GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY → npm run dev → http://localhost:3000. Optional MONGODB_URI for durable local data. Without an AI key, heuristics + protocol library still work.
home.da2: docs/STADIUM_CREDENTIALS.md. Quick: volunteer Alex Rivera + metlife_2026 + PIN WC26-MET. Ops Lead: metlife_2026 + ops_metlife_2026. Register needs no PIN; login after Ops approval requires the stadium PIN.
home.da3: src/lib/orchestrator.ts (A–D routing), src/lib/fan-assist.ts (voice + coaching), src/lib/xai.ts (Google AI Studio multi-model cascade), src/lib/protocol-pack.ts (seeded FAQs/emergencies), src/lib/memory.ts (Mongo memory). Canonical rules: prompts/system-prompt.md.
home.da5: Local default: data/db.json (gitignored). Production/Render: set MONGODB_URI (Atlas) — app_state + memory_events + mirrored collections. Tenancy always filters by stadium_id. POST /api/admin/reset reseeds. After protocol code changes, reset so new pack loads.
home.da6: A: Where is the nearest ADA elevator to Gate 3? / Where can I buy food near section 110? · B: Best restaurants near the stadium downtown? (out of scope → Fan Guide) · C: Low brochure inventory at Gate 1 · D: Missing child near Gate 3 / medical distress section 112 / unattended bag by restrooms.
home.readyBody: Use demo credentials above — locally (npm run dev) or on your Render URL with MongoDB connected.
home.footer: StadiaChat · FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium volunteer & Ops Lead prototype · Tenancy-enforced AI Core · GitHub + Render
home.jq6: Where does it run and is data durable?
home.ja6: Source on GitHub. Local: npm run dev with optional file DB. Production: deployed as a Next.js Web Service on Render with MongoDB Atlas (MONGODB_URI) so users, messages, and incidents survive restarts. Check GET /api/health/storage → active_backend mongodb. Demo credentials work on the live URL the same as local.
home.dq7: How do I deploy to Render?
home.da7: docs/DEPLOY_RENDER.md. Web Service (not Static): Build npm install && npm run build · Start npm start · Branch main. Env: MONGODB_URI (required), GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY (recommended), MONGODB_DB=stadiachat. Atlas Network Access 0.0.0.0/0. Verify GET /api/health/storage shows mongodb.
home.da8: docs/SETUP.md (local build), docs/DEPLOY_RENDER.md (Render), docs/FAQ.md (this FAQ in long form), docs/STADIUM_CREDENTIALS.md, docs/PROTOCOL_PACK.md, README.md. Secrets only in .env.local or Render Environment — never commit.
home.problemBody: FIFA World Cup 2026 venues will staff thousands of multilingual volunteers. Radios and group chats do not scale: noisy channels, no protocol library, weak tenancy, and slow escalation when a critical incident needs an Operations Lead. StadiaChat is a stadium-isolated AI ops interface that answers SOPs instantly, routes A–D correctly, deploys emergency protocols, and helps volunteers assist fans via mic and speaker — with a 300s safety override if the Lead does not respond.
home.criteria1Body: Clear WC2026 ops problem, non-goals, demo script, and category model (A–D) documented on this page and in docs/.
home.criteria2Body: HttpOnly signed sessions, stadium tenancy, role gates, rate-limited auth, protected admin reset, upload magic-byte checks, security headers.
home.criteria3Body: Domain modules (orchestrator, protocols, scope), typed API, serialized DB writes, compact protocol pack shared across venues.
home.criteria4Body: Labeled fields, password inputs, skip link, tab semantics, live error regions, reduced-motion, focus-visible styles.
home.criteria5Body: Protocol match before LLM, multi-model cascade with TTL, capped message polls, skip heavy Mongo mirrors on hot path.
home.criteria6Body: Automated unit tests for routing scope + rate limit; npm test / smoke scripts for regression.
home.whyBestTitle: Why this is the best fit
home.whyBestBody: Built for WC2026 matchday ops—not generic chat. Tenancy, A–D triage, emergency SOP deploy, Lead workflow, and Fan Voice together solve radio noise, SOP lag, and multilingual floor pressure. See docs/WHY_STADIACHAT.md and docs/FEATURES.md.
